title: openpilot 0.11.1 uses a VLM-assisted pipeline for better phone-use detection
friendlyTitle: openpilot uses AI labeling to spot unsafe phone use better
summary: comma.ai’s openpilot 0.11.1 release includes a new driver-monitoring model whose phone-use labels were generated with a locally hosted vision-language model, improving detection of active handheld phone use while reducing false positives on mounted phones.
friendlySummary: comma.ai says its new driver-monitoring model uses vision-language-model labels to better detect when drivers are using a phone.
whyItMatters: It is a concrete example of AI improving safety tooling through better labeling, not just end-user chat. The pattern matters for robotics, vehicles, and industrial systems where model quality depends on scalable, carefully checked training data.
friendlyWhyItMatters: This shows AI helping make safety systems more accurate, especially when good training labels are hard to collect by hand.
